highlights.title: Claude Code CVE-2026-39861: Sandbox Escape via Symlink
highlights.summary: A security vulnerability has been disclosed for Claude Code that allows a sandbox escape through symlink manipulation. If you are using Claude Code in a production or shared environment, you should review the advisory and update to a patched version immediately. This is a high-priority issue for any team relying on Claude Code's sandboxing for security isolation.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.136 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds two notable features: a new `settings.autoMode.hard_deny` option for auto mode classifier rules that unconditionally block certain actions regardless of user intent, and `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY_FOR_OTEL` to re-enable session quality surveys for enterprise users capturing telemetry via OpenTelemetry. Enterprise teams using Claude Code in automated pipelines should review these new controls for better governance and observability.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.137: VS Code Windows Activation Fix
highlights.summary: This patch release fixes a bug where the Claude Code VS Code extension failed to activate on Windows machines. If you are a Windows user who has been unable to get the extension running inside VS Code, updating to v2.1.137 or later should resolve the issue. Check your extension version in VS Code and update via the Extensions panel if needed.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest model in the Opus family, which powers Claude Code's most capable reasoning tasks. This is relevant for Claude Code users because model upgrades can improve code generation quality, multi-step reasoning, and agentic task performance. Check Anthropic's blog for details on capability improvements and any changes to API pricing or availability.

highlights.title: Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML
highlights.summary: This Hacker News discussion explores how Claude Code excels at generating plain HTML-based solutions, often producing surprisingly complete and functional results with minimal prompting. The linked examples demonstrate practical use cases where leaning into HTML output—rather than complex frameworks—can save significant development time. Beginners will find this a great reminder to start simple and let Claude Code handle the boilerplate.

highlights.title: Claude Code | I Wish I Knew These 7 Rules When I Started
highlights.summary: This YouTube video covers seven practical rules the creator wishes they had known when first using Claude Code, making it ideal for developers just getting started. The tips focus on real workflow improvements that can prevent common beginner mistakes and help you get better results faster. Watch this before diving into long sessions to build good habits from the start.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This concise YouTube tutorial is designed to get you productive with Claude Code quickly, covering the most important features and workflows in just 30 minutes. It's a great option for developers who want a fast but comprehensive overview without committing to a multi-hour course. Ideal for those who already have some coding experience and want to accelerate their Claude Code skills.

highlights.title: Show HN: I Built a 500K LOC Production App Alone in 7 Months
highlights.summary: A developer shares proof of building a 500,000-line production application solo in 7 months with the help of AI coding tools including Claude Code, complete with a 5-hour uncut live session as evidence. This is a compelling real-world case study of what's possible when AI assistance is integrated deeply into a solo developer's workflow. The linked article and video break down the strategies and tooling used throughout the project.

tips_of_the_day: Before starting a new Claude Code session on Windows, confirm your VS Code extension is updated to v2.1.137 or later — the previous version had an activation bug that silently prevented the extension from loading.

When working with Claude Code in auto mode for automated pipelines, use `settings.autoMode.hard_deny` to define a strict blocklist of actions that should never be executed automatically, giving you a safety net independent of prompt context.

Instead of jumping straight to a complex framework, try asking Claude Code to generate a plain HTML prototype first — it often produces a surprisingly complete result in one shot, which you can then iterate on or hand off to a more structured build.
